name: JPMorgan Chase
crisis_role: Acquired Bear Stearns (Mar 2008) and Washington Mutual (Sep 2008)

name: Citigroup
crisis_role: Largest US-bank write-downs; received TARP + government backstop

name: Goldman Sachs
crisis_role: Converted to bank holding company; took TARP; Buffett capital injection

name: Morgan Stanley
crisis_role: Near-failure; rescued in part by a US$9bn MUFG investment

name: HSBC Holdings
crisis_role: Large US subprime losses via Household Finance; 2009 rights issue

name: Barclays
crisis_role: Bought Lehman's US operations; raised Gulf sovereign capital

name: Deutsche Bank
crisis_role: Heavy trading/credit losses through 2008

name: UBS
crisis_role: Among the largest subprime write-downs; Swiss state stabilisation

name: ING Group
crisis_role: Received EUR 10bn Dutch state capital injection (Oct 2008)

name: Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group
crisis_role: Invested US$9bn in Morgan Stanley (Oct 2008)

name: Nomura Holdings
crisis_role: Acquired Lehman's Asia-Pacific and European/Middle-East operations

name: Mizuho Financial Group
crisis_role: Subprime-related losses; participated in Merrill Lynch capital raise

name: Industrial and Commercial Bank of China
crisis_role: Held US subprime securities; bought 20% of Standard Bank (~US$5.5bn)

name: Bank of China
crisis_role: Among the largest US subprime/MBS exposures of Chinese banks

name: Ping An Insurance
crisis_role: Wrote down its stake in Fortis (~EUR 2.3bn loss)

name: DBS Group
crisis_role: Singapore Lehman Minibond retail fallout; regional credit stress

name: ICICI Bank
crisis_role: Share-price slump and depositor-confidence scare (Oct 2008)

name: Shinhan Financial Group
crisis_role: Korean banks hit by acute USD-funding and won pressure (KDB-Lehman talks lapsed)

name: Standard Bank Group
crisis_role: ICBC took a 20% stake; JSE and rand sold off sharply

name: FBN Holdings (First Bank of Nigeria)
crisis_role: Operated through the 2008-09 Nigerian banking crisis (GFC + margin-loan/oil shock; CBN bank rescues 2009)
